transcript: we just took a family vacation to the most magical Resort in Hawaii here's everything you need to know there were eight of us in total and we stayed at the Hilton Hawaiian Village in aahu weiki the property was covered in gardens cultural activities and local Wildlife there were plenty of shops and places to eat we'd heard that check-in could be up to 2 hours so we booked a room in the alii tower which offers a private coner check-in and we were able to go right into our room for eight people we booked a two queen room a connecting parlor and a king room with two rollway beds the rooms were really nice they had extra amenities like a coffee maker and unlimited bottled water the bathrooms had large soaking tubs and good bath products and each bedroom as well as the Parlor had massive balconies overlooking the ocean the elite Tower comes with additional amenities like a dedicated gym and a pool reserved just for guests of this Tower you can see it pictured here beneath the balcony there was also an adults only pool that we did not visit but we did get to check out the tapa pool which was nice and open late and the super pool which is the largest in y ke Ki there's a massive Lagoon that you can play in we rented one of these giant bikes and it was $71 for an hour and it was really hard to drive this Resort is Disneyland level busy so it's like a competitive sport to find a chair next to the pool but the Family Pool did have multiple water slides and the kids loved it rooms here start around 200 a night for all three rooms for five nights we paid 5500 or just under 700 per person the sunsets and the nights were equally magical we loved it here I'll put details in the comments now let's get back to Vegas
